headlines.title: How will social media ban work and when does it start?
headlines.description: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

headlines.title: Russia was behind arson attacks targeting PM, BBC reveals
headlines.description: Evidence shows Russians directing the plot and stoking tensions with fake far-right and Muslim groups.

headlines.title: How soon could the economy go back to normal after a US-Iran deal?
headlines.description: Experts warn the impact of the war will continue to affect the global economy for months to come.

headlines.title: Boy, 2, seriously hurt in nursery playground car crash
headlines.description: A 63-year-old woman is arrested on suspicion of causing serious injury by dangerous driving.

headlines.title: Award-winning investigative journalist Roger Cook dies aged 83
headlines.description: The New Zealand-born reporter was credited with having invented the doorstep interview technique.

headlines.title: Man arrested for allegedly pushing woman in front of bus in 2017
headlines.description: A 44-year-old man is in custody over the incident where a woman appeared to be shoved into the path of a bus.

headlines.title: OnlyFans 'agents' control and threaten creators while taking half their earnings, BBC finds
headlines.description: Dozens of women on OnlyFans describe being exploited by managers who promised to help maximise profits.

headlines.title: Palestine Action ban is lawful, Court of Appeal rules
headlines.description: The Home Office had challenged a High Court ruling that the group's proscription should be quashed.

headlines.title: World Cup hydration breaks - who are the winners and losers?
headlines.description: They have become a regular feature at World Cup games - but who are the winners and who are losers of the mandatory hydration breaks?

headlines.title: Brazilian woman dies after rope-jumping instructors fail to attach cord
headlines.description: Three men have been arrested after instructors failed to attach a rope to her before helping her jump from a bridge.

headlines.title: Fifa seeks explanation over VAR official's hand gesture
headlines.description: Fifa is seeking an explanation over a hand gesture made by video assistant referee Shaun Evans before Germany's 7-1 win over Curacao on Sunday.

headlines.title: France star Mbappe vows to increase defensive work after criticism
headlines.description: The French captain has faced scrutiny over both his performances and leadership, but supporters say the criticism has gone "too far".

headlines.title: Graham Potter's journey from last-chance saloon to World Cup redemption
headlines.description: For a manager who was dismissed by West Ham in September after failing at Chelsea before that, few would have expected Graham Potter to be steering a nation to a winning start in the World Cup this summer.

headlines.title: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet
headlines.description: The BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman on the big changes coming down the line for young people online.

headlines.title: Unclear if US-Iran agreement will provide respite to Lebanon
headlines.description: Pakistan says the deal includes Lebanon, but recent ceasefires there have failed to take hold.

headlines.title: Why you might not be buying the right pain relief for period cramps
headlines.description: Many women are buying less effective pain medication for period cramps, supermarket data suggests.

headlines.title: Millions of people can get discounts on their bills - here's how
headlines.description: Lower social tariffs allow many people on benefits to get cheaper deals for water, broadband and phone.

headlines.title: Is the convertible heading into the sunset?
headlines.description: UK drivers have taken a shine to the SUV but could the fate of the convertible be reversed?

headlines.title: Jurors, lawyers and social workers face 'resilience test' in inaccessible courts
headlines.description: Access issues faced by wheelchair users in courts include broken lifts, “unsafe” ramps and unsuitable toilets.

headlines.title: Why I sold my business to my staff
headlines.description: As more US company owners reach retirement age many are selling up to their employees.

headlines.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
headlines.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

headlines.title: Trump and thousands of others watch UFC fight on White House lawn
headlines.description: Justin Gaethje beat Ilia Topuria to win the lightweight championship in the main event.

headlines.title: Canadian lynx one of big cat sightings in Welsh countryside
headlines.description: Fifteen "big cats" were reported to authorities in Wales between January 2020 and July 2025.

headlines.title: Australia demands answers after girl taken hostage is shot dead by Pakistan police
headlines.description: Pakistani police said the nine-year-old was mistakenly shot as they pursued a group of armed robbers.

headlines.title: New attempt to legalise assisted dying in England and Wales
headlines.description: Lauren Edwards says she will bring the exact same bill which was narrowly passed by the Commons but ran out of time in the Lords.

headlines.title: US musician Oliver Tree dies as two helicopters crash mid-air
headlines.description: The singer-songwriter is among six people presumed dead in air crash over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday.

headlines.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
headlines.description: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.

headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Royal Marines Take Control Of Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
headlines.description: Royal Marine Commandos boarded a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the English Channel.

headlines.title: The battle in rural America against AI data centres
headlines.description: Utah approves the world’s largest data centre (62sq miles) but opposition is growing

headlines.title: Inside Ella Toone’s life on and off the pitch

headlines.description: Fifa is seeking an explanation over a hand gesture made by video assistant referee Shaun Evans before Germany's 7-1 win over Curacao on Sunday.

headlines.title: McCullum worried for Stokes as coach gives no captaincy backing
headlines.description: Brendon McCullum refuses to be drawn on Ben Stokes’ future as England captain, but repeatedly speaks of his “worry and concern” for the all-rounder.

headlines.title: Stones goes from brink of retirement to World Cup 'pure joy'
headlines.description: John Stones' selection for England's World Cup squad may have raised a few eyebrows but the player says he had to "dig deep" to secure his place in the 26-man travelling party.

headlines.title: Cox and Baker to make Test debuts in second Test

headlines.title: France star Mbappe vows to increase defensive work
headlines.description: French captain Kylian Mbappe vows to increase his defensive efforts at the World Cup in the face of criticism.

headlines.title: Cucurella joins Real Madrid - will Fernandez follow?
headlines.description: Marc Cucurella has officially joined Real Madrid just hours before his opening World Cup game with Spain, raising the question of whether his former Chelsea team-mate Enzo Fernandez follows.
